exercises.title: Function with Docstring
exercises.description: Write a well-documented function with a proper docstring. Then call it and access its docstring.
exercises.starterCode:
def celsius_to_fahrenheit(celsius):
    """
    Convert a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit.

    Args:
        celsius: Temperature in degrees Celsius.

    Returns:
        Temperature in degrees Fahrenheit.
    """
    return (celsius * 9 / 5) + 32

# Call the function
print(celsius_to_fahrenheit(0))    # freezing point
print(celsius_to_fahrenheit(100))  # boiling point
print(celsius_to_fahrenheit(37))   # body temperature

# Access the docstring
print(celsius_to_fahrenheit.__doc__.strip().split('\n')[0])
exercises.expectedOutput:
32.0
212.0
98.6
Convert a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit.

exercises.title: Void Function (No Return Value)
exercises.description: A function that does not return a value (void function) implicitly returns None. Write a function that prints a formatted header, then call it.
exercises.starterCode:
def print_header(title, width=40):
    """Print a section header with a border."""
    print("=" * width)
    print(title.center(width))
    print("=" * width)

# Call the function
print_header("Student Report")
print_header("Summary", 20)

# Show that it returns None
result = print_header("Test")
print(result)
hints: print_header does not return a value, so assigning its result gives None.
.center(width) centers a string in a field of the given width.

exercises.title: Helper Functions
exercises.description: Break a program into small helper functions. Define three helpers — one to compute BMI, one to classify it, and one to display the result.
exercises.expectedOutput:
Weight: 70 kg
Height: 175 cm
BMI:    22.9
Status: Normal weight

warmup.title: Quick Recall: def and call syntax
warmup.description: def defines a function; calling it by name runs it. What does this print?
